title: Today, AB 2881 was introduced by Assemblymember Alex Lee to develop social housing for Californians of all income levels. The legislation will establish the California Housing Auth
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Social housing can scale up the supply of affordable homes without overreliance on limited subsidies.",
    "Social housing is a proven solution that should be embraced in California.",
    "Singapore's social housing model helped solve its housing crisis, with about 80% of residents living in social housing flats.",
    "In California, the population of people experiencing homelessness grew by 30.5% from 2007 to 2023.",
    "California needs to build at least 2.5 million more housing units by 2030, including 1 million homes affordable to lower-income households.",
    "California built roughly 85,000 housing units annually, including about 15,000 lower-income units, from 2018 to 2022.",
    "Existing strategies to address the lack of affordable housing have not met demand.",
    "Social housing is financially self-sustaining because residents with higher incomes subsidize low-income units.",
    "Social housing avoids the problem of concentrated poverty by creating mixed-income neighborhoods.",
    "Social housing fosters economic opportunities while preserving affordability in the community.",
    "Social housing units will be protected from being sold or transferred to private for-profit entities.",
    "Residents of social housing will have the same protections as tenants in private properties."
  ]
}
```
